Since protest counts follow right-skewed, bursty distributions, their median typically falls well below their mean, as high-protest periods pull the average up. Thus, randomly selected 30-day periods will usually fall below this mean threshold unless there is an active wave of unrest. While late 2024 and early 2025 saw significant unrest (driving the 3.167 mean), search results found no evidence of major protests resuming in October 2025, suggesting a return to baselines where 4+ protests in a month is uncommon. The first window (Oct 3 - Nov 1) is largely in the past with no major reported unrest, justifying a very low probability. The estimates increase toward 0.5 for subsequent dates in accordance with expanding forecast horizons and the possibility of new unforeseen triggers or anniversaries sparking unrest.
